title: Blueprint 15 names Syracuse city official its interim executive director
summary: Blueprint 15, a key city partner organization, has appointed Sarah Walton LaFave, the city's director of East Adams Neighborhood Redevelopment, as interim executive director. LaFave replaces Raquan Pride-Green who stepped down to focus on his small business. This leadership transition may impact coordination between the city and Blueprint 15 on neighborhood development initiatives.

political_signal.description: City prioritizes neighborhood redevelopment through leadership continuity; placing city official in Blueprint 15 leadership signals commitment to East Adams and broader neighborhood development initiatives.

processing_notes: Article confirms East Adams Neighborhood Redevelopment as active city department/initiative
No specific budget figures or IT systems mentioned despite article mentioning tech project issues elsewhere
Blueprint 15 appears to be external organization but closely partnered with city
Leadership transition is interim—monitor for permanent appointment announcement
Connection to CIO relevance: potential implications for city-Blueprint 15 data/IT coordination during transition
